reforms.description: The Swiss National Bank initiated a programme to provide preferential short-term export credits to the clock and watch making industry, as well as the textile and footwear industries, to support their export activities during the economic downturn.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance export activities, which can lead to increased economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was initiated and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a targeted support measure rather than a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The banks undertook to provide the clock and watch making industry and the textile and footwear industries with preferential short-term export credits.

reforms.description: The 1976 Confederation budget included a complementary budget designed to reflate the economy, providing financing appropriations totalling Sw. Frs. 600 million, which is expected to generate significant orders and impact employment positively. Additionally, the Swiss National Bank announced its readiness to help finance public investment by supporting the Confederation and Cantons in their 'reflation budgets' to stimulate economic activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding is aimed at stimulating economic activity and employment through significant public investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget and financing measures were included in the 1976 Confederation budget and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in public funding aimed at economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: This budget, which was put into effect at the beginning of 1976 and provides for financing appropriations totalling Sw. Frs. 600 million, should, according to official estimates, generate a volume of orders worth about Sw. Frs. 1 billion and, because of the nature of the projects which have been selected, its impact on employment should be significant.
